LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL FIVE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 4, 2018 AND THE COURT, PER CCP 128.7(C)(2) ISSUES AN ORDER FOR SYNERGY'S COUNSEL TO SHOW CAUSE IN WRITING NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 27, 2018 WHY THEY HAVE NOT VIOLATED CCP 128.7(B) BY FILING AND CONTINUING TO PROSECUTE SYNERGY'S CROSS-COMPLAINT (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR CSS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER ENTITLED "ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND CONTINUING HEARING ON DEMURRER" WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: M. GOODMAN; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
ORDER RE: CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT OF SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT (SEE SCANNED ORDER)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT. CROSS-DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL FIVE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL FIVE CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ARGUMENT THAT THE CROSS-CLAIMS MUST BE ARBITRATED NEEDS TO BE RAISED BY A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION BECAUSE OF THE POSSIBLE APPLICATION OF CCP 1281.2(C) WHICH REQUIRES EVIDENCE BY THE PARTIES BEARING ON THE EXERCISE OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION. ALL CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. HOWEVER, THE COURT'S OWN MOTION PER CCP 128.7 IS GRANTED AS TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE, AS SYNERGY ACKNOWLEDGES, IT CAN ONLY STATE A CLAIM FOR EXPRESS INDEMNITY AND IS NOT ABLE TO STATE A CLAIM FOR IMPLIED INDEMNITY. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT.

Cross-defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'s demurrer to all five causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Synergy Project Management is overruled as to all five cause of action. The argument that the cross-claims must be arbitrated needs to be raised by a motion to compel arbitration because of the possible application of CCP 1281.2(c) which requires evidence by the parties bearing on the exercise of the court's discretion. All claims are adequately alleged. However, the court's own motion per CCP 128.7 is granted as to the third and fourth causes of action because, as Synergy acknowledges, it can only state a claim for express indemnity and is not able to state a claim for implied indemnity.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Continued To Dec-04-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT.

Cross-Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'s demurrer to all five causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Synergy Project Management is continued to December 4, 2018 and the court, per CCP 128.7(c)(2) issues an order for Synergy's counsel to show cause in writing no later than November 27, 2018 why they have not violated CCP 128.7(b) by filing and continuing to prosecute Synergy's cross-complaint. The conduct of Synergy's counsel that appears to violate CCP 128.7(b) is the filing and continued prosecution of a cross-complaint that is barred by well-established law. CSS has no obligation to indemnify Synergy against claims brought by Mr. Johnston when CSS has provided workers compensation. "[A]n employer that provides compensation coverage has no further liability for workplace injuries to an employee. Therefore, if a nonnegligent third party pays damages for an employee's injuries that are attributable in whole or in part to the negligence of the employer, the Act's limitations on employer liability preclude the third party from obtaining equitable indemnity from the employer." (Privette v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689, 698 (citations omitted).) Contractual claims by third parties are likewise barred if they are collateral to or derivative of an injury covered by workers compensation. (Vacanti v. State Comp. Ins. Fund (2001) 24 Cal.4th 800, 814.) It appears that CSS has provided workers compensation coverage for Mr. Johnston, so it does not also need to indemnify Synergy for any of CSS's alleged negligence that contributed to the accident. Unless Synergy shows cause why it has not violated CCP 128.7(b) or dismisses the cross-complaint, on December 4, 2018 the court intends to dismiss the cross-complaint per CCP 128.7(d).

Counsel for CSS is required to prepare a proposed order entitled "Order to Show Cause and Continuing Hearing On Demurrer" which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
